body.content:
You don't often get email from desmond.chan@aquilaclouds.com. Learn why this is important
Cloud & AI Management Platform
 
 

Hi Adam,

I wanted to check in one last time to see if it makes sense for us to connect. I know AI and cloud cost management can be challenging, and we’ve helped many companies gain better visibility, reduce cloud billing, and eliminate unnecessary spend.

With our AI-powered FinOps platform, you can gain actionable insights, eliminate AI and cloud spend waste, and drive continuous monitoring and continuous optimization (CMCO), so your cost strategy stays efficient as you scale.

If you're interested, I’d be happy to walk you through how our AI-driven insights can help optimize your cloud spend. Just reply with a time that works for you, or click here to grab a slot on my calendar.

If now isn’t the right time, no worries, happy to reconnect whenever it makes more sense for you. 🙂

[GSE-142] - Tivoli Off-boarding to Veeam (Internal Project)

  Kristina Soriano  10:26 AM ET    

Project Updates Summary:

  • Commercial: Tivoli/Veeam SKU components mapping into Aptum Managed Backup SKUs for new servers have been agreed upon and finalized. Enablement of AMB SKU components are still being worked on as price matching is taking longer than expected.

  • Next Focus: Prioritize completing AMB SKUs enablement and documentation, close remaining technical gaps, drive customer follow-ups for CBT reboot/validation and tracking Tivoli decommissioning

data.subject: Expedient EMERGENCY Service Maintenance Notification - Elastic Cloud Enterprise Infrastructure Maintenance Change #627913

body.content:
Adam Nelson,

In an effort to maintain the highest quality of service, periodically it is necessary to conduct maintenance activities that affect our service infrastructure.

Please find the details of a scheduled maintenance event below.

Change Control #: 627913
Description: EMERGENCY Elastic Enterprise Cloud Infrastructure Maintenance
Date / Time: 2026-04-16 17:00:00 - 2026-04-20 12:00:00%

Because of timeout issues encountered during the completion of Change Control #623679, Expedient has opted to schedule an extended window to ensure that snapshot operations and upgrades have ample time to complete.

Maintenance Details: This emergency maintenance event has been scheduled to re-attempt an upgrade of the infrastructure supporting the Elastic Cloud Enterprise to the latest version. This is a continuation of work that was attempted in Change Control #623679. The Elastic Cloud Enterprise is used to deliver your Elastic as a Service, Expedient CTRL, and/or Elastic Security services.

Client Impact: There is no anticipated client impact for this maintenance event, as the Elastic Cloud Enterprise environment includes multiple redundant devices. Change activities inherently increase the risk of unanticipated impact, which is why we are providing you with this informational notification. The actual impact should not exceed the scheduled time frame.

We are committed to ensuring that both your service and our expertise are always available. For inquiries regarding this work, please submit a request to the Support Management Console (SMC) referencing the change number above. If you do not yet have access to the SMC, please contact our support center at 1-888-227-9400.

Thank you for your continued business.

Regards,

The Expedient Operations Support Center

Please note that all times listed above are Eastern Time. To convert to the local time of your affected service location, please reference the following link: https://support.expedient.com/help/timezone

You are receiving this email because you are a client contact for Aptum. If you don't want to receive this type of email in the future, please configure your email and recorded telephone message notification settings at https://support.expedient.com/settings. If you are having trouble seeing this email, you can view this notification online at https://support.expedient.com/notification/overview/id/62421.

© 2026 Expedient

To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30

 

Overview of Customer Footprint

  • The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
  • One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
    • Power requirements
    • Weight and handling constraints
  • Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
  • Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.

 

Customer‑Specific Observations

  • Digital AI
    • Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
    • Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
  • Cloudian
    • Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
    • Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
    • It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
  • Infinity
    • Uses one cabinet and office space.
    • Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
  • OpenText
    • Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
    • Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
  • Perforce
    • Only cage customer at Bracknell.
    • Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
  • Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
    • Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
    • Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.

 

Likelihood of Migration to Aptum

  • The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
  • A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
    • De‑racking
    • Transportation
    • Re‑racking
    • Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
  • Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.

 

Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs

  • Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
  • Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
  • Connectivity details:
    • Port speeds (1G, 10G)
    • Bandwidth utilisation
    • Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
  • IP address information:
    • IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
    • Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
  • Contact status:
    • Whether the customer has already been contacted
    • By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations

  • Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
  • A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
  • No major technical blockers were identified:
    • Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
    • Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
  • Follow‑up is required to:
    • Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
    • Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.

 

Customer Communications

  • Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
  • Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
  • It was agreed that:
    • Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
    • The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
    • Aptum will contribute language describing:
      • Facility capabilities
      • Migration assistance services
  • The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.

 

Key Agreements

  • No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
  • Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
  • The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
  • Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

 

 

Action Items

  • Sergio B
    • Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
    • Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
  • Aptum
    • Review the draft customer communication.
    • Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
  • Network & Facilities
    • Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
    • Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
  • All parties
    • Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

data.subject: Salon Connexion - Votre organisation est-elle vraiment prête pour l'IA ?

body.content:
You don't often get email from evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca. Learn why this is important
Si vous avez des difficultés à lire ce courriel cliquez ici
Bonjour Adam,
 
Le Salon Connexion 2026 revient avec une programmation taillée pour celles et ceux qui veulent dépasser les discours et voir l'IA à l'œuvre.

Nous avons sélectionné deux conférences incontournables pour vous : des études de cas concrets, des retours d'expérience sans filtre, et des leviers actionnables dès votre retour au bureau.
 
Le monde du travail se transforme à une vitesse fulgurante. Cette session dresse un état des lieux clair de l'intersection entre expérience employé, communication interne et intelligence artificielle. À travers des tendances clés et des cas d'usage concrets, découvrez comment réduire les frictions numériques, mieux connecter vos équipes en contexte hybride et bâtir une performance organisationnelle durable.

Cette conférence vous permettra de :
  • Comprendre les impacts concrets de l’IA sur l’expérience employé.
  • Identifier les principales frictions numériques à éliminer.
  • Structurer une approche durable autour de la communication interne pour plus de performance organisationnelle.
Trois projets d'IA, trois réalités d'entreprise, une vision pragmatique. Cette conférence met en lumière des cas réels touchant l'IA agentique, la vision par ordinateur, le traitement automatique du langage naturel et l'IA générative. Sans langue de bois : les défis rencontrés, les choix technologiques qui ont guidé le travail, et les résultats obtenus. Repartez avec des idées concrètes à explorer dans votre propre réalité.

Cette conférence vous permettra de :
  • Vous inspirer d'études de cas réels pour identifier des opportunités d'IA dans votre contexte

Hi,

As you know, Aptum is now working with an SFDC vendor who supports us with administrative work, projects, and platform improvements. The first phase focused primarily on cleaning up our SFDC instance, as there were many unused objects and code components. This phase was completed last week.

We have now moved into what is referred to as Camp Services. These are tasks typically handled by a standard SFDC admin and are included in our monthly fee. Any work outside of Camp Services is estimated using points, based on the vendor’s guidelines.

For each engagement, the first step is to confirm whether the request is included in Camp Services. If it is not, I ask the vendor to provide a point estimate for the specific request so we can avoid any unexpected charges.

For additional improvements, below is what we have identified so far. We need to assign an owner for each item—this person will be responsible for providing requirements—and, based on several variables, we will also need to assign a priority. Here is what I have so far.
 

Improvement
Owner
Comments
Identify the impact of our advisory efforts on future customer acquisition/growth


Look into the current process where renewal opportunities are automatically created and decide if it makes sense


Process to identify and work open opportunities where the close date is in the past


Improvements found in UAT


Remove unused columns from objects
Jorge
This is an ongoing task I will work on.

If you have additional improvements in mind, please reply to this email with the details.

I will call a meeting next week so we can go over this and decide on next steps.
